blocker_summary: Patched llc (LLVM 22.1.7 at H:\llvm-dxil\build\bin\llc.exe) has ZERO texture load/store intrinsic support: it rejects llvm.dx.resource.load.texture.2d and llvm.dx.resource.store.texture.2d with 'unknown intrinsic' errors and contains zero llvm.dx.texture.* strings in the binary, blocking the luminance_reduction pass from producing Texture2D/RWTexture2D DXIL artifacts.
investigation_methodology: Minimal .ll scratch cases (cases A-J): ten hand-authored LLVM IR modules in spike/godot-rurix/passes/luminance_reduction/artifacts/toolchain_probe/, each isolating a single intrinsic + target-ext-type combination, compiled with 'llc.exe <case>.ll -filetype=obj -o <case>.obj' and the exit code / stderr / obj_produced recorded in probe_results.json.

IntrinsicsDirectX.td source review: read H:\llvm-dxil\llvm-project\llvm\include\llvm\IR\IntrinsicsDirectX.td to enumerate every int_dx_resource_load_* / int_dx_resource_store_* definition, and read H:\llvm-dxil\llvm-project\llvm\lib\Target\DirectX\DXILResourceAccess.cpp createLoadIntrinsic (L389-420) and createStoreIntrinsic (L153-183) to inspect the C++ lowering dispatch for ResourceKind::Texture2D.

llc.exe binary findstr: ran findstr for 'llvm.dx.resource.' and 'llvm.dx.texture.' against H:\llvm-dxil\build\bin\llc.exe to enumerate which intrinsic name strings are physically present in the compiled binary, independent of the .td source.

cross-version llc survey: ran the case_A.ll (load.texture.2d) and case_H.ll (store.texture.2d) reject cases through EVERY DXIL-capable llc build on this workstation via cross_version_texture_intrinsic_probe.py, recording the per-build verdict in cross_version_probe_results.json. This isolates whether a newer/alternate llc would recognize the texture intrinsics.

cross_toolchain_confirmation.summary: The texture load/store intrinsics are rejected by EVERY DXIL-capable llc build on this machine, across two LLVM major versions. This proves the gap is an upstream DirectX-backend limitation (the intrinsics are not defined in any surveyed toolchain), not a regression specific to the patched 22.1.7 build that simply bumping to a newer llc would fix.
test_cases: case_A.ll (llvm.dx.resource.load.texture.2d, target("dx.Texture2D<float>", 0, 0)) -- the exact form rurixc emits for a Texture2D<f32> load
case_H.ll (llvm.dx.resource.store.texture.2d, target("dx.RWTexture2D<float>", 0, 0)) -- the exact form rurixc emits for a RWTexture2D<f32> store

llc_builds_surveyed.build_type: clean upstream, optimized build with assertions (llvm-project commit 82c5bce5233f964da4f8086b2341067314d841d7, 2026-06-24)

cross_toolchain_confirmation.interpretation: Even the bleeding-edge 23.0.0git upstream llc (roughly two LLVM releases ahead of the patched 22.1.7 DirectX build) still lacks int_dx_resource_load_texture* / int_dx_resource_store_texture*, so bumping to a newer llc does not unblock segment 4i. The required toolchain capability -- a llc whose DirectX backend defines AND lowers the texture load/store intrinsics rurixc emits -- does not exist in any locally available build. Producing a real runtime-mappable texture-capable DXIL container requires either landing the four upstream capabilities enumerated in patched_llc_capability_list, or bridging via dxc (graphics=B path) once texture lowering exists upstream.

non_determinism_note: llc 22.1.7 has a non-deterministic crash in the 'DXContainer Global Emitter' pass (memory-safety bug, Exception Code 0xC0000005 access violation). Cases C, D, I, and J flip between crash and accept across runs: in this probe run cases C and D accepted (obj produced, exit 0) while cases I and J crashed (exit 3221225477). On a subsequent run cases C/D may crash and cases I/J may accept. This non-determinism is itself toolchain-blocker evidence: even the 'accepted' intrinsics cannot be relied upon to produce objects consistently. The crash occurs regardless of whether the texture handle is used, and also affects dx.TypedBuffer / dx.RawBuffer handles when left unused (cases E, F, G).

accepted_intrinsics.name: llvm.dx.resource.load.rawbuffer
accepted_intrinsics.evidence: Case J (load.rawbuffer + target("dx.RawBuffer", float, 0, 0)): NON-DETERMINISTIC. In this probe run obj_produced=false, exit=3221225477 (crash in DXContainer Global Emitter). In other runs the intrinsic is accepted and obj is produced. The intrinsic IS recognized by name (present in llc.exe binary findstr as string #9 and defined as int_dx_resource_load_rawbuffer in IntrinsicsDirectX.td); the crash is a downstream emitter bug, not an intrinsic-name rejection.

accepted_intrinsics.name: llvm.dx.resource.load.typedbuffer
accepted_intrinsics.evidence: Case C (load.typedbuffer + target("dx.TypedBuffer", float, 0, 0, 0)): obj_produced=true, exit=0 (accepted this run). Case D (load.typedbuffer + target("dx.Texture2D<float>", 0, 0)): obj_produced=true, exit=0 (accepted this run; IR-level selection does not cross-validate resource kind against intrinsic name). Both cases produced .obj files, proving the typedbuffer load intrinsic is recognized by llc.

accepted_intrinsics.name: llvm.dx.resource.store.rawbuffer
accepted_intrinsics.evidence: No dedicated scratch case in this probe run. The intrinsic IS recognized by name: present in llc.exe binary findstr (string #12) and defined as int_dx_resource_store_rawbuffer in IntrinsicsDirectX.td. Distinction: recognized-by-name is NOT the same as obj-produced; this case was not exercised end-to-end. Not rejected as 'unknown intrinsic'.

accepted_intrinsics.name: llvm.dx.resource.store.typedbuffer
accepted_intrinsics.evidence: Case I (store.typedbuffer + target("dx.TypedBuffer", float, 1, 0, 0)): NON-DETERMINISTIC. In this probe run obj_produced=false, exit=3221225477 (crash in DXContainer Global Emitter). The intrinsic IS recognized by name (present in llc.exe binary findstr string #13 and defined as int_dx_resource_store_typedbuffer in IntrinsicsDirectX.td); NOT rejected as 'unknown intrinsic'. The crash is a downstream emitter bug. Distinction: recognized-by-name is NOT the same as obj-produced.

rejected_intrinsics.name: llvm.dx.resource.load.texture.2d
rejected_intrinsics.exact_error:
H:\llvm-dxil\build\bin\llc.exe: error: H:\llvm-dxil\build\bin\llc.exe: H:\rurix\spike\godot-rurix\passes\luminance_reduction\artifacts\toolchain_probe\case_A.ll:24:27: error: unknown intrinsic 'llvm.dx.resource.load.texture.2d'
  %r = call { float, i1 } @llvm.dx.resource.load.texture.2d(target("dx.Texture2D<float>", 0, 0) %h, i32 0, i32 0)
                          ^
rejected_intrinsics.evidence: Case A (load.texture.2d + target("dx.Texture2D<float>", 0, 0)): exit=1, obj_produced=false. Also reproduced in Case B (load.texture.2d + target("dx.Texture2D<float>") without trailing 0,0 params): same 'unknown intrinsic' error, exit=1, obj_produced=false. This is the form rurixc currently emits.

rejected_intrinsics.name: llvm.dx.resource.store.texture.2d
rejected_intrinsics.exact_error:
H:\llvm-dxil\build\bin\llc.exe: error: H:\llvm-dxil\build\bin\llc.exe: H:\rurix\spike\godot-rurix\passes\luminance_reduction\artifacts\toolchain_probe\case_H.ll:25:13: error: unknown intrinsic 'llvm.dx.resource.store.texture.2d'
  call void @llvm.dx.resource.store.texture.2d(target("dx.RWTexture2D<float>", 0, 0) %h, i32 0, i32 0, float 1.0)
            ^
rejected_intrinsics.evidence: Case H (store.texture.2d + target("dx.RWTexture2D<float>", 0, 0)): exit=1, obj_produced=false. Mirrors Case A for the store direction; both load and store texture intrinsics are equally unsupported.

dx.Texture2D<float>.evidence: target("dx.Texture2D<float>", 0, 0) is not registered as a valid target-ext type. When a handle of this type is allocated but left unused (Case E: handlefrombinding only), llc crashes in 'DXContainer Global Emitter' with Exception Code 0xC0000005 (exit 3221225477). When the same type is used with a recognized intrinsic (Case D: load.typedbuffer), IR-level selection does NOT cross-validate the resource kind and obj is produced, but this does not imply the type is registered.
dx.Texture2D<float>.contrast: Contrast with dx.TypedBuffer and dx.RawBuffer, which ARE registered target-ext types (Cases C, J use them as handle types).

dx.RWTexture2D<float>.evidence: target("dx.RWTexture2D<float>", 0, 0) is not registered as a valid target-ext type. Used in Case H (store.texture.2d) but rejected at the intrinsic-name level before the type-registration path is reached. No scratch case isolates the RWTexture2D type-registration crash because the store.texture.2d intrinsic is rejected first.
dx.RWTexture2D<float>.contrast: Contrast with dx.TypedBuffer (writable variant target("dx.TypedBuffer", float, 1, 0, 0)) and dx.RawBuffer, which ARE registered.

dx.TypedBuffer.evidence: target("dx.TypedBuffer", float, 0, 0, 0) is registered. Case C uses it as the handle type and obj is produced (exit 0). Case G (handlefrombinding only, unused) still crashes the DXContainer Global Emitter, indicating the crash is not specific to texture types but affects unused handles of any target-ext type.

dx.RawBuffer.evidence: target("dx.RawBuffer", float, 0, 0) is registered. Case J uses it as the handle type. Registration form documented in docs/DirectX/DXILResources.rst.

missing_intrinsics: int_dx_resource_load_texture* (no load.texture.2d, load.texture.3d, load.texture.2dms, etc. defined)

createLoadIntrinsic.behavior_for_Texture2D: ResourceKind::Texture2D hits reportFatalUsageError("Load not yet implemented for resource type")

createStoreIntrinsic.behavior_for_Texture2D: similar stub for ResourceKind::Texture2D (no real lowering; reportFatalUsageError path)

binary_findstr_texture_count.interpretation: ZERO strings matching llvm.dx.texture.* found in llc.exe. This is the binary-level confirmation that no texture intrinsic exists in the compiled toolchain, independent of the .td source review. Combined with the .td source review (no int_dx_resource_load_texture* / int_dx_resource_store_texture* definitions) and the scratch-case rejections (Cases A, B, H), this is a three-way confirmation that texture load/store intrinsics are entirely absent from patched llc.

patched_llc_capability_list.required_signature: { float, i1 } <- [target("dx.Texture2D<float>", 0, 0), i32, i32] (handle, coord_x, coord_y)
patched_llc_capability_list.rationale: Defines the intrinsic name so llc stops rejecting llvm.dx.resource.load.texture.2d as 'unknown intrinsic'. Maps to DXIL opcode textureLoad (66).

patched_llc_capability_list.required_signature: void <- [target("dx.RWTexture2D<float>", 0, 0), i32, i32, float] (handle, coord_x, coord_y, value)
patched_llc_capability_list.rationale: Defines the intrinsic name so llc stops rejecting llvm.dx.resource.store.texture.2d as 'unknown intrinsic'. Maps to DXIL opcode textureStore (67).

patched_llc_capability_list.capability: Register target-ext types dx.Texture2D<float> and dx.RWTexture2D<float>
patched_llc_capability_list.location: H:\llvm-dxil\llvm-project\llvm\lib\Target\DirectX (target-ext type registration; refer to docs/DirectX/DXILResources.rst for the dx.TypedBuffer / dx.RawBuffer registration form)
patched_llc_capability_list.required_signature: target("dx.Texture2D<float>", 0, 0) and target("dx.RWTexture2D<float>", 0, 0) registered as valid target-ext types
patched_llc_capability_list.rationale: Currently unregistered, causing the DXContainer Global Emitter to crash (Exception Code 0xC0000005, exit 3221225477) whenever a handle of these types is allocated, even when unused (Cases E, F). Registration is required for any texture-handle-bearing module to reach obj emission.

patched_llc_capability_list.capability: Implement C++ lowering for texture load/store
patched_llc_capability_list.required_signature: For ResourceKind::Texture2D: replace reportFatalUsageError("Load not yet implemented for resource type") stub with real lowering that emits dx.op.textureLoad (opcode 66) / dx.op.textureStore (opcode 67) DXIL opcodes
patched_llc_capability_list.rationale: Even after the intrinsic name is registered in .td and the target-ext type is registered, the C++ lowering currently stubs out Texture2D with a fatal error. Without real lowering, the intrinsic will compile past name lookup but fail at DXIL opcode emission.

resolution.summary: The texture load/store TOOLCHAIN blocker is resolved. The self-invented forms documented above (llvm.dx.resource.load.texture.2d / store.texture.2d + target("dx.Texture2D<float>", 0, 0)) are abandoned; rurixc now emits the UPSTREAM DirectX target-ext + intrinsic spelling, which a locally-patched llc lowers to dx.op.textureLoad(66) / dx.op.textureStore(67) and the DXC validator accepts. This resolves the toolchain gap only; see remaining_blockers_not_toolchain for why the canonical provenance switch is NOT performed.

achieved_form.note: The achieved form is the UPSTREAM spelling, not the self-invented dx.Texture2D<float> spelling this blocker and texture_artifact_provenance_policy were written against (see the provenance-policy errata).
achieved_form.target_ext_srv: target("dx.Texture", float, 0, 0, 0, 2)  (Texture2D<f32> SRV; ElemTy=float, IsWriteable=0, IsROV=0, IsSigned=0, Dim=2)
achieved_form.load_intrinsic: float @llvm.dx.resource.load.level(<handle>, <2 x i32> coords, i32 mip, <2 x i32> offsets) -> dx.op.textureLoad(66)
achieved_form.store_intrinsic: void @llvm.dx.resource.store.texture(<handle>, <2 x i32> coords, float value) -> dx.op.textureStore(67)

load_side.detail: Texture load.level is merged upstream (llvm/llvm-project PR #193343). The clean 23.0.0git build at H:\llvm-clean-82c5bce5-build\bin\llc.exe already contains llvm.dx.resource.load.level and lowers it to dx.op.textureLoad(66).

store_side.detail: Texture store had no upstream intrinsic (tracked by llvm/llvm-project issue #194930). Implemented locally: int_dx_resource_store_texture intrinsic + DXILOp<67, textureStore> + DXILOpLowering::lowerTextureStore (coords -> Coord0..2; value splatted across Val0..3 with write mask 15) + the DXILResourceAccess getpointer/store transform. Additionally folds insertelement-built dynamic coordinates directly into the dx.op scalar args and erases the dead coordinate vector, so dynamic (SV_DispatchThreadID) texel coordinates validate.

evidence.stage0_load_probe: artifacts/toolchain_probe/texture_load_stage0_probe_results.json (case_K.ll: upstream load.level ACCEPT + byte-stable x8; self-invented case_A/case_H still reject_unknown_intrinsic)
evidence.store_probe: artifacts/toolchain_probe/case_L.ll: load.level + store.texture ACCEPT, byte-stable x8, DXV 'Validation succeeded', dx.op.textureLoad(66)+textureStore(67) present in container
evidence.rurixc_golden: tests/dxil/cs_texture.rx (1D index form) + tests/dxil/cs_texture_2d.rx (2D method-call form): rurixc emits the upstream form; .dxil-disasm goldens carry dx.op.textureLoad(66)/textureStore(67) after DXV acceptance
evidence.luminance_kernel: src/lib_texture.rx compiles (cargo run -p rurixc --target dxil, RURIX_LLC + RURIX_DXC_DIR set) to a DXV-validated DXIL container with real 2D dx.op.textureLoad(66)/textureStore(67) coordinates (src.load(sx,sy) / dst.store(x,y,avg))

remaining_blockers_not_toolchain: math_pyramid_parity: src/lib_texture.rx is a single-level (level-0) reduction. Full Godot luminance parity requires the multi-level pyramid cascade + EMA feedback + previous-luminance double buffering + final-level WRITE_LUMINANCE clamp/min/max gating (all intentionally deferred; see lib_texture.rx header and offline_compile_evidence.json known_gaps). runtime_mappable stays false until math parity is proven.

runtime_dispatch_proof: runtime_mappable=true (revert condition 2) additionally requires a real D3D12 compute dispatch smoke (probe next_action provide_grx009_luminance_real_d3d12_dispatch_smoke), a separate slice beyond offline compilation.

canonical_switch.reason: texture_artifact_provenance_policy.md Revert/Re-cut requires ALL FOUR conditions. Condition 1 (patched llc supports texture load/store intrinsics + target-ext types) is now SATISFIED (via the upstream form). Conditions 2-4 are NOT: runtime_mappable stays false (math parity + D3D12 dispatch not proven), so provenance stays rurix_owned=false, pass_manifest is unchanged, and bridge tracked digests are unchanged. Fail-closed: canonical artifacts remain on the raw-buffer/hlsl_bridge fallback.
